topic: 在不加入新功能的基础上完善和稳定 Fabric v1.7.0，并将 AI 客户端范围收敛到 Claude / Codex / Cursor

relevant_files.annotation: Defines WindsurfWriter (L125-136), RooCodeWriter (L138-149), GeminiCLIWriter (L151-164). Removing these three classes is the central excision point — ClaudeCodeCLIWriter / CursorWriter remain, CodexTOMLConfigWriter lives in toml.ts.

Imports the 3 retired writers (L7), constructs them in resolveClients (L70-89), produces DetectedClientSupport rows for Windsurf/RooCode/GeminiCLI (L158-196). resolveClients becomes shorter; detectClientSupports loses 3 of its 8 entries; the windsurfDetected/rooDetected/geminiDetected locals (L109-111) go away.
ClientKind union L1-8 currently lists 'Windsurf' | 'RooCode' | 'GeminiCLI'. Removing these three literals is a typed source of truth — TS will surface every consumer that branches on these kinds.

BootstrapClient union L12 includes the three retired clients. CLIENT_ALIASES L42-61 maps 6 client surface names. mapClientKind switch L181-199 and mapBootstrapClientToClientKind switch L201-216 both branch on retired kinds. Whole module needs a 3-client trim.

CLIENT_ALIASES L14-33 lists 7 alias entries for the 3 retired clients (windsurf, roocode/roo-code/roo, geminicli/gemini-cli/gemini). parseClientFilter is the user-facing --clients parser used by both `fab config install` and tests.

ClientPaths L1-9 declares windsurf?/rooCode?/geminiCLI? — public API surface of fabric.config.json. Trimming requires a config schema migration note (or stays backward-compatible by ignoring unknown).
Zod clientPathsSchema L5-13 mirrors the public ClientPaths fields. Whatever is removed from types/config.ts must also be removed here, otherwise Zod will silently accept retired keys.

Public JSON Schema published with the package; declares clientPaths.windsurf (L33-37), clientPaths.rooCode (L38-42), clientPaths.geminiCLI (L43-47). External users referencing $schema must see consistent removal here.

L40 'No bootstrap targets...' enumerates 'claude,cursor,windsurf,roo,gemini,codex'. L51 / L54 still mention 'cursor,codex,gemini'. After the trim every example must read 'claude,cursor,codex' to stay accurate.
L39, L49, L52 contain the Chinese mirror of the same outdated example lists. zh and en strings must be edited together to keep i18n.test.ts snapshots aligned.

Repo-root template referenced ONLY by docs/RULE_REGISTRY.md L90; bootstrap-guide.ts (L13-17) only loads templates/agents-md/variants/{cocos,vite,next}.md so this file is no longer wired into init/bootstrap. Safe to delete after rule registry update.
Same status as GEMINI.md — referenced only in docs/RULE_REGISTRY.md L91. Unreferenced by runtime code paths.
Same status — referenced only in docs/RULE_REGISTRY.md L92. Unreferenced by runtime code paths.

Duplicated copy that ships in the published @fenglimg/fabric-cli npm tarball (package.json `files: ['dist','templates']`). DIVERGES from repo-root copy (lacks `<!-- fab:rule-id -->` headers). Must be deleted to avoid shipping references to retired clients in the public artifact.
Same as above — duplicated and divergent copy in published bundle.
Same as above — duplicated and divergent copy in published bundle.

L90-92 enumerate the 3 retired bootstrap rule-ids (`bootstrap/gemini`, `bootstrap/roo`, `bootstrap/windsurf`). After the trim these rows must be removed; otherwise the registry will reference deleted templates.
L10 lists the six clients that may be used for local validation. Reduce to 'Claude Code、Cursor、Codex CLI'.
L36 mentions 'GEMINI.md' as a file the bootstrap stage no longer emits. L441 has a 'Gemini CLI' row in the client capability table. Update prose + table.

L11 describes Fabric as 'an MCP-first, cross-client AGENTS.md protocol for six AI clients: Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, Roo Code, Gemini CLI, and Codex CLI'. Project tagline must change to three clients.

Historical mentions only (L118 'no longer emits root-level AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, or GEMINI.md'; L186 lists Gemini CLI in the v1.1 client list). Should NOT be retroactively edited; instead, the next release entry should explicitly call out the client-scope reduction.

L23-25 explicitly asserts parseClientFilter('cursor,codex,gemini') resolves to {Cursor, CodexCLI, GeminiCLI}. After removal this expectation must be replaced with 'cursor,codex' → {Cursor, CodexCLI}, and the test name corrected.

L72 asserts existsSync(target/GEMINI.md) === false. The assertion is fine, but its rationale should now be the strong claim 'GEMINI.md must never be referenced'. Useful as a regression guard once trim is complete.

init.ts contains NO direct references to windsurf/roo/gemini — it routes through resolveClients() and DetectedClientSupport. So trimming touches it indirectly only via the 3-client TypeScript surface. Confirmed by grep returning zero matches.

821-line monolith — central health check used by both `fab doctor` and dashboard /api/doctor. Stability work concentrates here: meta/event-ledger/forensic/initContext/ruleSections/ruleTestIndex inspections. Catch blocks at L301, L314, L344, L435, L453, L480 — verify all paths surface actionable error.
Thin wrapper that delegates to runDoctorReport / runDoctorFix. Strict mode (L59) makes any warning fail. Add fixable codes only on the server side (doctor.ts L260-282).

Append-only ledger writer L21-38 uses raw appendFile (NOT atomicWriteText). Concurrent fabric processes (CLI + MCP server) writing to .fabric/events.jsonl could interleave bytes on some filesystems. Worth documenting and/or wrapping in a single-process serializer.

atomicWriteText (L12-17) used for derived files (agents.meta.json etc.) but ledger uses appendFile. Boundary between 'snapshot files: atomic' and 'append-only files: non-atomic' should be made explicit in code comments.

Centralized HTTP error normalizer (L61-129) — does string-prefix matching on error.message ('Path escapes project root:', 'Cannot find human lock entry:', 'Cannot find ledger entry:'). Brittle: any reword of those messages elsewhere silently degrades 403/404 responses to 500.

MCP server entry — registers plan-context + rule-sections tools and bootstrap README resource. startStdioServer L92-97 has no top-level error boundary beyond the IIFE catch. Resource handler L74-86 throws raw fs.readFile errors back to MCP if README is missing.

Single CI gate: pnpm install → pnpm -r build → tsc --noEmit → pnpm -r --if-present test → lint-protected-tokens → NO_COLOR snapshot rerun. No client-scope contract test (would catch reintroduction of retired clients).
Pre-commit hook is minimal — only `node scripts/lint-stdio.mjs`. No staged-file typecheck, no targeted vitest run; relies on CI for breakage detection.
Snapshot test covering scan/translation outputs (snapshot file is __snapshots__/i18n.test.ts.snap). Snapshots will need refresh after the i18n string trim.
Good unit coverage for runDoctorReport / runDoctorFix paths. Used as a regression baseline before any change to inspection helpers.

patterns.pattern: Client kind dispatch via switch statements
patterns.concern: Two parallel switches that must stay in sync with ClientKind union and BootstrapClient union — duplicate case-list maintenance.
Two near-identical alias tables in two command modules — same surface names mapped to different value spaces (BootstrapClient vs ClientKind). High drift risk; trimming requires editing both.
5 of these JSON writers differ only in `clientKind` literal and `defaultPath()` body. Could collapse into a single configurable JsonClientConfigWriter — unlocks easier scope changes.

resolveClients and detectClientSupports each contain hand-rolled per-client blocks. Two arrays-of-clients drift apart easily. After trim, both must be edited; could be unified into one ClientDescriptor[] table.

Two divergent template trees (`diff` shows packaged copy lacks `<!-- fab:rule-id -->` headers). Neither tree is referenced by bootstrap-guide.ts at runtime — bootstrap-guide.ts only loads templates/agents-md/variants/*.md. These bootstrap-prefixed templates are LEGACY/UNREFERENCED but still ship.

patterns.pattern: Error message string-prefix matching for HTTP status mapping
examples: packages/server/src/api/_error.ts:93-115 ('Path escapes project root:', 'Cannot find human lock entry:', 'Cannot find ledger entry:')
patterns.concern: Brittle coupling — any rewording in services/* will silently degrade 403/404 responses to a 500. Should switch to typed error classes.

patterns.pattern: Append-only ledger uses non-atomic appendFile while snapshot writes use atomicWriteText
patterns.concern: Mixed write semantics. Multi-writer scenarios (CLI doctor --fix while MCP server is also up) can cause partial-line interleaving in events.jsonl. No file-locking documented. Doctor parseable check (L431) catches but does not auto-recover.
User-facing CLI error messages cite 'cursor,codex,gemini' as the canonical example list. After scope trim these strings teach users an invalid alias and break i18n snapshot.

packages/cli.role: User-facing CLI binary `fab` / `fabric`. Owns init wizard, bootstrap install, MCP client config writers, doctor delegate, hooks installer, scan/forensic.
key_files: src/commands/init.ts (1897 lines — orchestrates scaffold + 3 stages: bootstrap/mcp/hooks)
src/commands/doctor.ts (101 lines — thin delegate to server doctor)
src/bootstrap-guide.ts (the only bootstrap template loader, framework-driven)
client_specific_files: templates/bootstrap/{GEMINI.md, roo-fabric.md, windsurf-fabric.md} (legacy packaged templates — DELETE)
templates/claude-hooks/, templates/claude-skills/, templates/codex-hooks/, templates/codex-skills/ (Claude/Codex-only assets — KEEP)

packages/server.role: MCP server (stdio + HTTP/Express). Owns rule-meta builder, doctor service (the heart of health checks), event ledger, audit log, plan-context / rule-sections / get-rules services, and HTTP API endpoints consumed by the dashboard.
key_files: src/index.ts (MCP server bootstrap, exports doctor + rule-meta + ledger constants)

packages/dashboard.role: Read-only Preact + Vite + Tailwind4 SPA bundled into the server. Pure viewer — never mutates state; CLI is the writer.

packages/shared.role: Pure types + Zod schemas + i18n catalogs. Re-exports through `./types`, `./i18n`, `./node` subpath exports.
key_files: src/schemas/ (11 schema modules incl. agents-meta, forensic-report, rule-test-index, event-ledger-event)

doc_files_to_edit: CHANGELOG.md (add new release entry — do NOT rewrite history)

test_coverage_gaps.missing: resolveClients + detectClientSupports have NO direct unit test. Coverage is only via init-mcp-scope.test.ts and config-install.test.ts side effects.
installBootstrap is exercised only via init-claude-install.test.ts L66-74. parseClientFilter (private) untested; mapClientKind / mapBootstrapClientToClientKind untested except indirectly.
No writer unit tests for these three retired classes. Codex writer DOES have tests at config-install.test.ts L28-114. Claude/Cursor writers untested directly.

normalizeApiError relies on string-prefix matching with no test for any of the 3 prefixes ('Path escapes project root:', 'Cannot find human lock entry:', 'Cannot find ledger entry:'). High regression risk.
Has a test (event-ledger.test.ts) but no concurrency / partial-write recovery test. Doctor's parseable check at services/doctor.ts:431 is the only safety net.
Resource handler at L67-87 throws raw fs.readFile errors. No test asserting MCP-friendly error payload when README is missing.

Has multiple test files (init-*.test.ts ×9) but the orchestrator is a long monolith — refactor without tests of plan/wizard/scaffold seams is risky. No contract test ties detected clients → installed assets.

doctor_checks: createForensicCheck — .fabric/forensic.json present + parses + reports framework (manual: forensic_missing/forensic_invalid)
createMetaCheck — .fabric/agents.meta.json present + zod-valid + revision matches built revision (fixable: agents_meta_missing/agents_meta_stale; manual: agents_meta_invalid)
createRuleContentRefCheck — every meta node's content_ref resolves to a file under .fabric/rules/ or bootstrap README (manual: content_ref_outside_rules/content_ref_missing)
createRuleSectionsCheck — every .md under .fabric/rules/ parses via parseRuleSections (manual: rule_sections_invalid)
createRuleTestIndexCheck — .fabric/rule-test.index.json present + zod-valid + matches built index (fixable: rule_test_index_missing/rule_test_index_stale; manual: rule_test_index_invalid)
createEventLedgerCheck — .fabric/events.jsonl exists + writable + every line parseable (fixable: event_ledger_missing; manual: event_ledger_not_writable/event_ledger_invalid)
doctor_fix_actions: writeDefaultBootstrap (services/doctor.ts:665) writes a hardcoded one-line README (loses framework variant content)
ensureEventLedger (services/doctor.ts:671) creates empty file via writeFile + flag:'a'
ci_gates: GitHub Actions CI: pnpm -r build (tsup compile across cli/server/shared/dashboard)
GitHub Actions CI: pnpm -r exec tsc --noEmit (cross-package typecheck)
GitHub Actions CI: pnpm -r --if-present test (vitest suites)
GitHub Actions CI: node --experimental-strip-types scripts/lint-protected-tokens.ts (forbidden token rename guard)
GitHub Actions CI: NO_COLOR=1 rerun of @fenglimg/fabric-cli vitest for snapshot stability
ci_gate_gaps: No staged-file lint / typecheck in lefthook — every developer-side break is only caught remotely
No JSON-schema validation gate (schemas/fabric-config.json could drift from packages/shared/src/schemas/fabric-config.ts; not asserted in CI)
No client-scope contract test — adding a writer back without removing it would not be caught by any current assertion
No release.yml dry-run validation in PR CI; release flow lives in .github/workflows/release.yml not exercised by PRs

error_handling_smells.smell: swallowed catch: returns null without logging — debugging stale event reads is hard
same swallowed catch pattern, separate function
parseEventLedgerLine returns null on any parse failure — invalid lines are silently dropped (not surfaced as warnings up to doctor)
tryBuildRuleMeta swallows ALL errors and returns null; loses signal about why building failed
string-prefix matching for HTTP status mapping; rewording any of 3 messages elsewhere silently downgrades to 500
readPackageName catches all errors and returns undefined — invalid package.json silently treated as nameless project
Non-atomic appendFile to events.jsonl while other state writes use atomicWriteText — inconsistent durability promise
MCP bootstrap README resource throws raw fs error to client when missing — should map to a clean MCP error code

code_quality_smells.smell: 1897-line orchestrator file mixes plan-building, wizard, scaffolding, claude/codex hook installers, taxonomy generation. Hard to test in isolation; high blast radius for any edit.
Two divergent template trees, neither referenced by bootstrap-guide.ts at runtime. Likely legacy from pre-MCP era. Confirmed via diff: packaged copy lacks `<!-- fab:rule-id -->` headers.
Two near-identical CLIENT_ALIASES maps (different value spaces). Consolidate into a single alias→ClientKind table in shared.
Per-client class fan-out — ClaudeCode/Cursor/Windsurf/Roo/Gemini writers differ only in literal + defaultPath. Could be table-driven.

naming_inconsistencies: fab vs fabric: package.json declares both bin names ('fab', 'fabric'); i18n strings sometimes say 'fabric' (e.g., en.ts:217 'cli.scan.report.title' = 'Fabric scan report') and sometimes 'fab' (e.g., en.ts:228 'L0: Run fab init...'). User-facing inconsistency.
ClientKind 'GeminiCLI' vs alias 'gemini-cli' vs config field 'geminiCLI' — three casings of the same identity (acceptable, but documented nowhere)
rooCode (config field) vs RooCode (ClientKind) vs roo (BootstrapClient + alias) — three case variants for one client

questions_for_user: Removal vs deprecation: 是直接删除 Windsurf/Roo/Gemini 的 writer/类型/schema/templates，还是保留代码但加 `@deprecated` + 在 detectClientSupports 中标记 detected=false？删除更干净（- 200~250 行净减），保留则向旧用户的 fabric.config.json 兼容（zod 的 .strict() 会拒绝多余字段，但当前 schema 默认是 passthrough，所以保留也不强制阻断）。
fabric.config.json 老字段如何处理：clientPaths.{windsurf,rooCode,geminiCLI} 已写入用户文件的，是 (a) 直接报错（zod strict）、(b) 静默忽略 + 一次性 doctor warning、还是 (c) doctor --fix 自动剥离这三字段？建议 (b) → 下个 minor 改为 (a)。

doctor --fix 范围：当前只 fix 6 个 code（bootstrap_missing/event_ledger_missing/agents_meta_missing/agents_meta_stale/rule_test_index_missing/rule_test_index_stale）。是否扩展以下？(1) writeDefaultBootstrap 当前只写一行 README，应该改为调用 buildFabricBootstrapGuide（就地利用 framework 检测）；(2) 增加 client_scope_drift 自动剥离 retired clientPaths；(3) 增加 events.jsonl 部分写入修复（截断到最后一个完整行）。
lefthook 是否扩展为 staged-file vitest？目前 pre-commit 只跑 lint-stdio。代价：每次提交多 5~15s；收益：本地拦住 99% 的回归。
client-scope 守护是否需要 contract test？示例：vitest test 列表 ClientKind 的所有可能值并断言 === ['ClaudeCodeCLI','ClaudeCodeDesktop','Cursor','CodexCLI']，防止回归再加入 Windsurf/Roo/Gemini。建议 YES。
JsonClientConfigWriter 5 个子类是否合并为单个 table-driven writer？如果走删除路线，剩 ClaudeCodeCLI + Cursor 两个 JSON writer，合并价值降低；如果走保留路线，合并能让 scope 调整更容易。
templates/bootstrap/ 与 packages/cli/templates/bootstrap/ 两份模板都不被 bootstrap-guide.ts 使用，是否一并删除？还是先确认有没有外部脚本/文档引用？grep 范围内（除 docs/RULE_REGISTRY.md）未发现引用。
README + docs/initialization.md 文案：『支持 6 个客户端』改成『支持 3 个一线客户端：Claude / Codex / Cursor』；是否一并加一段『为何收敛』的设计说明？

_metadata.scan_strategy: Layer 1 breadth-first across packages/{cli,server,shared,dashboard} + templates/ + docs/ + schemas/ + .github/. Targeted ripgrep for the 3 retired client identifiers (case-insensitive, multi-spelling regex), then per-hit Read for context. Verified bootstrap-guide.ts loader to confirm templates/bootstrap/* are unreferenced. Cross-checked test files for assertions that pin retired clients.
_metadata.confidence: high (exhaustive on client_scope_map; medium on stability signals because a deeper read of init.ts internals was deferred to keep this exploration breadth-first)
